title: Russia-Ukraine War Update: Ceasefire Collapses, Deep Strikes
description:
The Easter ceasefire in the Russia-Ukraine war has ended, and this rapid war brief breaks down the most important confirmed developments from the last 12 hours. If you want a clear, high-credibility battlefield update focused on military relevance rather than headlines, this video covers what changed, what was struck, and why it matters.

This update begins with the collapse of the ceasefire. Russia announced the truce, but Ukraine's General Staff reported 10,721 Russian violations, including more than 1,000 artillery strikes and thousands of drone attacks. Russia, in turn, claimed 6,558 Ukrainian violations. The central takeaway is clear: the ceasefire failed, and active combat operations have resumed.

The video then covers Russia's immediate return to large-scale drone warfare. Overnight, 98 drones were launched. Ukrainian air defenses reportedly intercepted 87 of them, an 89 percent intercept rate, but nine still penetrated defenses. One confirmed strike hit an energy facility in Chernihiv, cutting power to 12,000 residents. That matters because attacks on energy infrastructure are designed not only to damage the grid, but also to strain civilian resilience and force resource diversion behind the front.

On the Ukrainian side, the brief highlights a deep strike inside Russia. Geolocated footage reportedly confirms fires at the Cherepovets Azot chemical plant in Vologda Oblast, more than 1,200 kilometers from the front line. Geolocated footage means visual evidence has been matched to a specific real-world location, making the claim more credible. The plant produces ammonia, which is a key chemical precursor used in the production chain for explosives. If damage is confirmed at scale, the strike could carry operational significance beyond symbolism by affecting industrial support for Russia's war effort.

The briefing also points to a major political development in Europe: Viktor Orban's loss in Hungary. In plain terms, this could weaken Russia's most reliable veto-friendly position inside the European Union. While this is not a battlefield event, it matters strategically because EU unity influences sanctions, aid, and the broader political environment surrounding the war.

Key developments covered in this brief:
- The Easter ceasefire collapsed, with both sides accusing each other of thousands of violations.
- Russia launched 98 drones overnight, with most intercepted but some getting through.
- A confirmed strike on Chernihiv energy infrastructure left 12,000 residents without power.
- Ukraine appears to have struck the Cherepovets Azot chemical plant deep inside Russia.
- Political shifts in Hungary may reduce Russia's leverage inside the European Union.

The brief closes by identifying what to watch next: possible Russian follow-on strikes, satellite confirmation of the Cherepovets fire, and any front-line movement near Lyman. Those indicators will help show whether this is simply a return to routine combat tempo or the start of a broader new phase after the failed truce.

If you found this helpful, consider subscribing for more clear, fact-focused war updates.

title: Sudan: 3-Year-Old Conflict has become a cycle of endless suffering- Press Conference| United Nations
description:
The UN humanitarian coordinator for Sudan called the three-year-old conflict there “an abandoned crisis,” warning that the scale of atrocities has yet to move the world to act.

Briefing reporters in New York, UN Resident and Humanitarian Coordinator for Sudan Denise Brown said the situation in the country has become a cycle of suffering with no end in sight.

“We really are on repeat in Sudan - repeats of sexual violence, repeated displacement, repeated deaths,” Brown said. “It feels like we're stuck in this cycle, and everything is being repeated.”

Brown said the Office of the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights, OHCHR, had documented patterns of sexual violence, citing a recent MSF report for Darfur entitled “No Safe Space Left for Women and Girls in Darfur.” The organization has treated close to 2,500 survivors of sexual violence over the past year.

“What we also need to be addressing here is the impregnation of these women and girls, and the consequences for them, for the families, for the communities, and of course most particularly for these children,” she said.

Brown said OHCHR had also documented mass atrocities around El-Fasher, where 6,000 people were killed in three days - a figure she said was likely an undercount. She noted that the UN Independent Fact-Finding Mission for Sudan now refers to the hallmarks of genocide.

“Why isn't the world outraged enough to do something about it? What has to happen more? What more has to happen for everyone to sit up and pay attention, to find a solution,” she said.

Brown, speaking from Khartoum, said the humanitarian community was present across the country but was not the solution. “We are here picking up the pieces,” she said, calling on member states to examine the war economy and the flow of weapons into the country.

She flagged Dilling as an active area of concern, saying the town has been under daily attacks since she visited in March, with convoys unable to get in and residents trying to flee again. She said she was also heading to the Blue Nile, where close to 30,000 people had been displaced.

On funding, Brown said the humanitarian response plan was only 16 percent funded against a 2.9 billion dollars ask for 2026, after closing 2025 at 35 percent. “When we get a tent, that tent, … doesn't become six tents. That tent is one tent,” she said.

“Please don't call this the forgotten crisis,” Brown also stated her personal plea. “I'm referring to this as an abandoned crisis.”

title: The Young Women of South Sudan are RESILIENT and need your SUPPORT
description:
In South Sudan, young women pursue education despite displacement, insecurity, and limited resources. Each lesson learned moves her forward. Each challenge overcome strengthens her path toward leadership, independence, and possibility.

But her journey is fragile.

Around the world, too many girls in conflict affected communities are being forced to pause or abandon their education. Research from UN Women makes one truth clear. When women and girls remain in school, entire communities grow stronger through healthier families, greater opportunity, and more resilient societies.

Right now, sustained support is urgently needed to ensure young women can continue their studies without interruption. Through PASS USA, we provide scholarships, housing, mentorship, and community support so that education remains a promise, not a risk.

Education does not just change her life.
It changes her family.
Her community.
Her nation.

Her journey cannot pause here.

Stand with her. Lift her forward. Help ensure her journey continues.

description: Since April 2023, Sudan has faced the world's largest humanitarian crisis: 150,000 dead, 14 million displaced, 70% of hospitals destroyed. While other conflicts dominate headlines, Sudan's war continues in silence. This song is for the forgotten—not to point fingers, but to open eyes. Knowledge is the first step toward change. #aicreator #aimusicvideo #sudan #legonews #goviral #viral

title: Abyei Peace Deal 2026: How Local Mediation Is Preventing Conflict Between Sudan and South Sudan
description:
In April 2026, the Ngok Dinka and Misseriya communities signed a landmark agreement to remove unauthorized weapons from one of Africa’s most disputed regions—caught between Sudan and South Sudan for over 15 years.


Facilitated by UNISFA, this deal highlights a powerful shift: local communities stepping in where national politics have stalled.


But the risks are real—armed group infiltration, military tensions, and shrinking humanitarian funding threaten to unravel progress.


In this episode, we explore:

  •  How community mediation is reducing violence in Abyei
  •  Why seasonal migration talks are key to peace
  •  The growing security threats in the region
  •  What this means for the future of conflict resolution

Can grassroots peace building succeed where formal peace processes have failed?

title: Russia is Losing the War in Ukraine.
description:
What Russia Should Have Taught Trump
Putin’s catastrophic losses in Ukraine are a warning about what ground troops in Iran could mean for America.

Russia has now lost more than 900,000 troops killed or wounded in Ukraine. That is a staggering number. To put it in perspective, that is more than the United States lost in the entirety of World War One.
And Russia was fighting in a country right on its border with established supply lines and familiar terrain.
Iran is none of those things.
Iran has 88 million people, a landmass three times the size of Ukraine, and mountain terrain that has historically made ground invasion a nightmare for any foreign power.
If the Trump administration is seriously considering a ground operation in Iran, the Russian experience in Ukraine should be required reading. Because Putin thought his war would last weeks. It has now lasted more than four years and cost Russia a generation of young men.
The math does not change just because the flag does.

Find me on all platforms at quietcanines. Links in bio.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

title: Something NIGHTMARISH Just Happened to Russia in Ukraine Putin Never Saw This Coming
description:
Something NIGHTMARISH Just Happened to Russia in Ukraine 
Fifty-two drones. Fifty two Russian attack drones were sent to destroy a single Ukrainian tank. Every single one of them failed. The tank survived, its crew walked away without a scratch, and then they drove it to a new position and started firing again. And that's not even the craziest thing that happened to Russia this week. Because while that unkillable tank was shrugging off an entire day's worth of Russian drone attacks, another Ukrainian tank crew, operating a German-built Leopard 2A6, pulled off a shot so impossibly accurate that it just shattered a record that has stood since the 1991 Gulf War.

title: ‘Disaster’ for Putin as Orbán ousted in Hungarian election landslide | Russia-Ukraine war latest
description:
Is Hungary turning away from Viktor Orbán’s “illiberal democracy”, or can we expect more of the same policies on the war in Ukraine?
Today, as Ukraine and the wider world react to the apparent rejection of Viktor Orbán’s model of “illiberal democracy” in Hungary, we ask: who is Péter Magyar, and what does his rise mean for Hungarian politics and Europe? As Hungarians confront a potential new political era, we examine whether real change is coming in Budapest – and how the European Union will handle the unresolved вопрос of the €90 billion loan for Ukraine. We also assess how much fighting actually paused during the Orthodox Easter ceasefire in Russia’s war against Ukraine, and ask whether Vladimir Putin is ultimately a winner or loser from the recent global attacks on oil infrastructure.

description:

description:
Why does a nation as impoverished as Yemen keep global superpowers awake at night? Behind the haze of conflict lies the strategic "jugular" of Bab el-Mandeb, where even the slightest tremor can choke the flow of the entire global economy. In this Geographic Knowledge journey, Planet Expedition takes you deep into the Sada mountains to decode the asymmetric power of those "in sandals" and marvel at thousand-year-old mud fortresses. title: Yemen, Houthis, Iran, and the Red Sea
description: In the midst of escalating tensions, the Yemen conflict has taken a perilous turn as the wider Iran–US–Israel discord intensifies. The Houthi movement, deeply entrenched in regional disputes yet maintaining strategic independence, has initiated limited missile strikes against Israel. This escalation raises concerns over potential retaliatory actions that could further strain Yemen’s fragile state. As Israeli and US operations target Iranian interests, fears mount that Yemen might once again become a central battlefield, exacerbating an already dire humanitarian situation. The Houthi's threats to disrupt Red Sea shipping, including the crucial Bab al-Mandeb Strait, underscore the broader implications—heightening global economic risks and complicating diplomatic resolutions. With Yemen on the brink, the international community remains watchful over a conflict that holds profound consequences for the region and beyond.

title: HOUTHI MISSILES & DRONES SHAKE ISRAEL #Israel #Houthi #MiddleEast #BreakingNews #JagatViralita
description:
🚀 Middle East tensions have escalated again after Yemen's Houthi group reported a direct attack on Israel, targeting Ben Gurion International Airport on Saturday night, April 4, 2026, at approximately 10:15 p.m. local time. The attack reportedly involved ballistic missiles and a swarm of combat drones.
Source: reuters.com

⚠️ Houthi military spokesman Yahya Sarea claimed the operation was a "joint attack" carried out in solidarity with the conflict in the region. Warning sirens sounded in Greater Tel Aviv, forcing thousands of residents and airport passengers to rush to shelters.
Source: aljazeera.com

🛡️ The Israeli military (IDF) confirmed the missile launches from Yemen. Air defense systems successfully intercepted most of the threats, although one projectile fragment reportedly landed in an open area around the airport perimeter without causing major damage.
 Source: bbc.com

✈️ Although there was no significant damage, flight operations were temporarily suspended for security reasons. This incident immediately sparked global concern, with several airlines reviewing routes to Tel Aviv.
Source: cnn.com

🌍 Analysts consider attacks on civilian airports like Ben Gurion a serious escalation that could trigger a broader military response from Israel and its allies, as well as increase the risk of a larger regional conflict.
Source: ft.com

title: Statecraft with Geeta Mohan: Bab el-Mandab Threat, Houthi Strikes, Global Trade at Risk Now
description:
While the world is fixated on the Strait of Hormuz, another critical chokepoint is quietly heating up—Bab el-Mandab. This narrow passage between Yemen and the Horn of Africa carries nearly 12% of global trade, connecting the Red Sea to the Suez Canal. And now, it is under growing threat.
#babelmandab #houthis #redsea #globaltrade #oilcrisis #middleeastconflict #iranproxy #straitofhormuz #shippingcrisis #geopolitics #energysecurity #statecraft #geetamohan #worldnews
The Houthis, who control much of Yemen’s coastline, are armed with Iranian-supplied missiles, sea mines, and fast attack boats. They may not fully shut down Bab el-Mandab—but they don’t need to. A strategy of “blockade light” is enough to shake global markets.

Intelligence assessments suggest a 70–80% probability of intensified drone and missile attacks within 48 to 72 hours of a Hormuz blockade. The targets? Saudi and UAE oil shipments—hit indirectly, but with maximum economic pressure.

A full closure remains unlikely. Why? Because it would trigger massive retaliation similar to Operation Prosperity Guardian, where US and UK forces already destroyed over 20 Houthi boats. The Houthis are calculated, not reckless. Their goal is disruption without inviting overwhelming force.

title: France: Lafarge Convicted in Syria Terror Finance Case 🚨
description:
A French court has convicted cement giant Lafarge for financing armed groups, including Islamic State, during the Syrian war. The company faces a €1.125 million fine, and its former CEO received a six-year jail sentence.  

This conviction stems from Lafarge's payments of nearly €5.6 million to groups, including Islamic State and Nusra Front, to ensure its Syrian plant remained operational between 2013 and 2014. The company had invested significantly in Syria just before the civil war began. The French ruling follows a 2022 case in the United States where the company pleaded guilty to similar charges and paid a substantial $778 million fine. Judges emphasized that these payments amounted to a 'genuine commercial partnership' with the Islamic State. This landmark case sets a significant precedent, marking the first time in France that an entire company has been tried for financing terrorism, underscoring evolving corporate accountability in conflict zones.

title: رسم الثورة السورية / رسم علم سوريا يرفرف / رسم عن الثورة السورية
description:
مبروك انتصار الشعب السوري، وقد عبرت عن مشاعري كالتالي: ✌️
📌 رسم الثورة السورية
📌 رسم علم سوريا يرفرف
📌 رسم عن الثورة السورية

سوريا عام 2024: عام غيّر كل شيء في دولة مزقتها الحرب
يحتفل السوريون بنهاية نظام الأسد، على الرغم من التحديات المستمرة والدمار الواسع النطاق في جميع أنحاء البلاد.

دمشق، سوريا – في عام 2024، أصبح سقوط نظام الأسد الذي طال أمده، والذي حكم سوريا لأكثر من 50 عاماً، نقطة تحول عالمية.

تلاشت الحرب السورية من عناوين الأخبار، لكنها عادت إلى الظهور بعد أن أطاح هجوم خاطف ببشار الأسد وأعاد الأمل للكثيرين في الشهر الأخير من العام.

وفي بداية عام 2024، قدرت منظمة الصحة العالمية أن أكثر من 65 بالمئة من السكان يحتاجون إلى مساعدات إنسانية.

أدى الزلزال الذي وقع في فبراير/شباط 2023 والذي دمر شمال سوريا إلى تفاقم الأزمة دون تحفيز دعم دولي إضافي.

وتضاعفت أسعار المواد الغذائية في عام 2024 مقارنة بعام 2023، وانخفضت قيمة العملة المحلية إلى واحد على 15 من قيمتها في عام 2020.

هاجمت إسرائيل سوريا في عام 2024، فدمرت مباني بأكملها في دمشق ومحافظات أخرى، واستهدفت مسؤولين رفيعي المستوى من إيران وحزب الله، وتسببت في إغلاق متكرر لمطاري حلب ودمشق.

استمرت الاحتجاجات السلمية في جنوب وشمال غرب البلاد، حيث احتج نشطاء في السويداء على سوء الأوضاع المعيشية وطالبوا بإسقاط نظام الأسد.

وواصل نظام الأسد وحلفاؤه قصف المناطق التي تسيطر عليها المعارضة، مما أدى إلى مقتل وجرح المدنيين.

دفع التهديد المستمر من النظام في الشمال الغربي هيئة تحرير الشام وائتلاف من جماعات المعارضة المسلحة إلى إطلاق "عملية ردع العدوان" في 27 تشرين الثاني/نوفمبر، مما أدى إلى تحقيق تقدم سريع في غرب حلب والاستيلاء على المدينة في غضون يومين.

واستمر التقدم عبر جنوب إدلب وحماة ودرعا وحمص حتى وصل في 8 ديسمبر/كانون الأول إلى دمشق مع فرار بشار الأسد إلى روسيا.

واندلعت الاحتفالات في جميع أنحاء سوريا، على الرغم من استغلال إسرائيل للوضع لضرب مواقع أمنية ومستودعات أسلحة وشن عملية توغل داخل سوريا من خلال الزحف عبر الخط الحدودي في مرتفعات الجولان.

ومع تقدم مقاتلي هيئة تحرير الشام، فتحوا أبواب سجون الأسد، وأطلقوا سراح الآلاف، وسلطوا الضوء على العدد الهائل من الأشخاص الذين اختفوا في "مسالخه البشرية".

بدأت الجهود لتحديد مكان حوالي 130 ألف سجين ومختفي قسريًا، ولكن كما اكتشفت آلاف العائلات، فإن البحث سيكون طويلًا ومرهقًا.

وكان الآلاف من النازحين داخلياً الذين اضطروا للفرار من البلاد يأملون في العودة إلى ديارهم، لكن الدمار الذي أحدثه الأسد كان واسع النطاق لدرجة أن بعض الناس لم يتمكنوا حتى من تحديد مكان منازلهم.

لا يزال عدم اليقين والخوف من المستقبل سائدين بين السوريين، لكن هناك إجماع على أن المستقبل يحمل وعوداً أكثر من الماضي.

مع انتقال العالم من عام إلى آخر، ينتقل السوريون من عام 1970 إلى عام 2025، حيث يتركون سنوات الأسد وراءهم.

title: "Trained Abroad…" Syria Foils Hezbollah Plot To Blow Up "Religious Figure" Outside Church | VERTEX
description:
Syrian security forces dismantled a Hezbollah-linked assassination plot in Damascus on April 12, 2026, arresting a five-person cell that had been surveilling a religious figure near a church in the city's historic Bab Touma Christian quarter — one of Damascus's oldest neighborhoods, where a strike carries symbolic weight far beyond a single target. Authorities say the cell received specialized military training abroad, and preliminary investigations confirmed direct links to Hezbollah. It marks the second time in under two months that Damascus has broken up an alleged Hezbollah cell on Syrian soil, following a February operation in the Mazzeh district where weapons were said to have come directly from the group. The pattern is significant — Syria's new post-Assad authorities have explicitly rejected Iranian influence and cut ties with Hezbollah, yet the old networks appear to still be operating inside the country. With Hezbollah simultaneously fighting Israel on the Lebanese front since March 2, the alleged Damascus plot suggests the group has not relinquished its reach inside what was once its most important corridor — even as the government that protected it has completely collapsed.

title: 中国が黒幕か？ クーデターと虐殺で血に染まるミャンマー内戦、その原因に怒り爆発
description:
ミャンマーの内戦は遠い国の悲劇ではありません。難民問題、周辺国への影響、中国の一帯一路と軍部支援、そして日本で暮らす私たちにもつながる現実まで、この一本で全体像をつかめます。


ミャンマーの内戦は、もはや国内問題だけではありません。中国にとってミャンマーは、インドを牽制し、海へ抜けるための重要な戦略拠点です。一帯一路、港湾、鉄道、エネルギー輸送路――そのすべてがミャンマーと深く結びついています。だから中国は軍政を支えながら、同時に反政府勢力にも接触するというしたたかな動きを見せています。なぜ中国はそこまでミャンマーに執着するのか。なぜ市民の反中感情はここまで強いのか。そしてミャンマーの未来は米中対立の中でどこへ向かうのか。クーデターの歴史だけでなく、その背後で動く国際政治まで一気に理解できる内容です。

title: AA Forces Pressure Junta Soldiers Punished as Min Aung Hlaing Blames 2020 Election
description:
Evening News (Apr 11) - AA Forces Pressure Junta Soldiers Punished as Min Aung Hlaing Blames 2020 Election

Myanmar’s civil war continues to intensify as frontline battles in Rakhine State take a dramatic turn.

Seven junta officers have reportedly been sentenced to death after retreating under pressure from AA forces.

Myanmar economic outlook 2026, conflict impact on economy Myanmar, Rakhine instability economic effects, Sagaing conflict economic disruption, investor confidence Myanmar decline, political uncertainty Myanmar economy, business risk Myanmar 2026, supply chain disruption Myanmar, logistics challenges Myanmar, inflation pressure Myanmar, cost of living increase Myanmar, labor market instability Myanmar, workforce displacement Myanmar, rural economy challenges Myanmar, agriculture disruption Myanmar, financial system Myanmar risks, banking sector uncertainty Myanmar, domestic investment slowdown Myanmar, foreign investment risk Myanmar, regional trade Myanmar instability, cross border trade challenges Myanmar, infrastructure risk Myanmar conflict, humanitarian crisis economic burden, poverty increase Myanmar, unemployment rise Myanmar, informal economy growth Myanmar, market instability Myanmar, long term growth challenges Myanmar, economic resilience Myanmar, Southeast Asia economic impact Myanmar

Meanwhile, Min Aung Hlaing shifts blame over the country’s crisis, sparking further controversy.

The situation in Myanmar continues to deteriorate as fierce clashes erupt in Rakhine State, particularly in the Buthidaung region. According to emerging reports, members of the Myanmar military who were forced to retreat under pressure from the Arakan Army (AA) are now facing severe consequences. Among them, at least seven officers have reportedly been sentenced to death and transferred to Sittwe Prison.

This development highlights growing tensions and internal disciplinary actions within the military as battlefield setbacks increase. The AA has been intensifying its operations across Rakhine State, placing significant pressure on junta forces and reshaping control dynamics in the region.

At the same time, Myanmar’s military leader Min Aung Hlaing has once again addressed the country’s political crisis, attributing the unrest and nationwide instability to alleged irregularities in the 2020 general election. His remarks have drawn criticism as the humanitarian and security situation across the country continues to worsen.

This video covers the latest updates on the Myanmar civil war, military developments, political tensions, and the broader implications for the region.

title: Myanmar energy crisis: US-Iran war forces fuel prices & inflation soaring
description:
Fuel shortages and long queues across Myanmar have left drivers struggling to access limited supplies as prices more than double. The crisis, driven by import dependence and rising global tensions, has worsened inflation and disrupted transport, emergency services, and humanitarian aid, deepening hardship for an already vulnerable population.

Al Jazeera’s Jack Barton reports.
